text: Exercise performance is trivially reduced during the early follicular phase of the menstrual cycle compared to all other phases.
The largest performance difference across menstrual cycle phases occurs between the early follicular and late follicular phases, with performance lowest in the early follicular phase.
The early follicular phase has the highest likelihood of being the worst phase for exercise performance, while all other menstrual cycle phases perform similarly.
specific_value: SUCRA: early follicular 30%, all other phases 53-55%

text: Both endurance- and strength-based exercise performance show trivially reduced outcomes during the early follicular phase of the menstrual cycle.
Due to large between-study variation and low-quality evidence, menstrual cycle-based training adjustments should be individualized rather than following general population-level guidelines.
The menstrual cycle effect on performance is too small to warrant avoiding key training sessions or competitions in any specific phase for most women.
